summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a spring‑'91 Shoreline show with smoking Truckin’ > New Speedway Boogie and stellar Jerry‑Bruce interplay.
review: The recording is a pristine SBD mix that lets the band’s chemistry shine – Hornsby’s keyboards, Jerry’s soaring lead, and Bruce’s piano drive every jam.  Highlights include a blistering Truckin’ → New Speedway sequence, a seamless Foolish Heart → Saint of Circumstance transition and a killer Drums‑Space‑Wheel segment, while the first set builds nicely with Desolation Row and Jack Straw.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 enthusiastic reviews consistently give five stars, praising both performance and sound quality; the weighted average of show (≈4.7) and recording (≈4.8) yields a strong 4.6 rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, lush mix with very clear instrument separation; a few reviewers note minor post‑drums clipping but overall the SBD is described as superb.

poor_songs: Cold Rain & Snow (feedback issues on some tracks)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds nicely with strong storytelling songs (Desolation Row, Jack Straw) and solid jams; the second set is a high‑energy marathon with seamless transitions—pre‑drums combos flow into an explosive Truckin’/New Speedway sequence and a powerful drums‑space‑wheel climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring lead work, flawless tone, and vocal strength; his interplay with Bruce is highlighted as a peak of the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Solid, melodic bass lines noted especially on Cold Rain & Snow and during the drums‑space segment.
band_member_comments.Bob: Energetic rhythm guitar and fired‑up vocals add extra drive; praised for enthusiasm throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Hornsby’s chemistry with Garcia is repeatedly lauded – sweet licks, tasteful fills and a mix that lets his keyboards shine; Vince also gets credit for tasteful accents.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (Mickey) delivers ferocious, extended drumming; the Drums‑Space‑Wheel segment is called “killer.”
